logMealSheetLoggedPhotos: {count, plural, one {Logged 1 photo ({kcal} kcal)} other {Logged {count} photos ({kcal} kcal)}}
heartRateChartFatBurnMinutes: {minutes, plural, one {1 min in fat-burn zone} other {{minutes} min in fat-burn zone}}
scoreExplainAiPicksATrafficLight: AI picks a traffic-light grade for each meal based on your personal health goals.
scoreExplainRatingsArePersonalised: Ratings are personalised to your goals, intolerances and eating history.
scoreExplainChronicLowGradeInflammation: Chronic low-grade inflammation from diet is linked to metabolic disease, joint pain, and cognitive decline.
scoreExplainAimForADailyAverage: Aim for a daily average below 4. Anti-inflammatory foods score 1–3; high-inflammation foods score 7–10.
scoreExplainGlycemicLoadCombines: Glycemic load combines carbohydrate quantity and quality. It predicts how much a meal raises blood sugar.
scoreExplainImportantIfYouHaveDiabetes: Important if you have diabetes, insulin resistance, or are managing energy levels.
scoreExplainFodmapsAreShortChain: FODMAPs are short-chain carbohydrates that are poorly absorbed and fermented by gut bacteria.
scoreExplainOnlyRelevantIfYouHaveIbs: Only relevant if you have IBS or a diagnosed gut condition. Safe to ignore otherwise.
scoreExplainWeUseTheNovaClassification: We use the NOVA classification system developed at the University of São Paulo.
scoreExplainLargePopulationStudies: Large population studies link ultra-processed food consumption to cancer, cardiovascular disease, and early mortality.
scoreExplainWhoRecommendsAdults: WHO recommends adults limit added sugar to < 25 g/day (5% of total energy).
scoreExplainThatIsAboutPctDay: That is about {pctDay}% of WHO's 25 g/day limit. Added sugars drive dental decay, insulin spikes, and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease.
scoreExplainAddedSugarIsThe: Added sugar is the single strongest dietary predictor of metabolic syndrome in Western diets.
scoreExplainScoreDetailUnavailable: Score detail unavailable for this meal.
scoreExplainEachMealGets: Each meal gets a 1–10 health score based on nutrient density, processing level, and alignment with your goals.
scoreExplainDailyAverageAbove6: A daily average above 6 is associated with better long-term metabolic health.
healthMetricsCardConnectAGlucoseMonitor: Connect a glucose monitor via Health Connect
healthMetricsCardInsulinDeliveryData: Insulin delivery data from connected devices will appear here
healthMetricsCardBloodGlucoseReadingsWil: Blood glucose readings will appear here
cardioHistoryImportFromStravaPeloton: Import from Strava, Peloton, Garmin, Apple Health, or Fitbit to see your history here.
